claim: Independent cross-check confirms the explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshot audit that theoretically detects project file mutations and blocks PRD readiness, but stress testing with real full-tool Claude agents has not been performed.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() function captures SHA256 hashes of all project files except those in allowed_write_roots, using git ls-files when available and falling back to filesystem walk.
evidence.why: This is the core before/after snapshot mechanism that establishes the baseline for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshots and returns array of changes with path and change type (added/modified/deleted).
evidence.why: This function detects any mutations that occurred during agent execution.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) before executing any evidence agents to establish baseline.
evidence.why: Establishes the before-state before agents can potentially mutate files.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot after all agents complete, then diffs snapshots and populates result.boundary with project_mutation status, allowed_write_roots, and changes array.
evidence.why: Detects and reports any file mutations that occurred during agent execution.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations are mapped to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, extended into readiness.blockers array, and force prd_ready=false when changes exist.
evidence.why: Violations automatically block PRD readiness regardless of agent results.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates agent writing unexpected-project-write.txt and verifies blocked status with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary audit blocks PRD readiness when mutations occur.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' verifies git ls-files integration detects untracked files written by agents.
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary audit works with git repositories.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' verifies file walker fallback excludes allowed_write_roots from mutation detection.
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary audit works without git and respects allowed_write_roots.

evidence.summary: Claude agent configuration sets claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools to empty strings and claude_no_session_persistence to true, avoiding tool restrictions.
evidence.why: Evidence dispatch relies on boundary audit for enforcement, not Claude tool allowlists.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness function validates evidence agent results, processes blockers, and integrates with readiness assessment.
evidence.why: Router integrates boundary violations into final readiness assessment.

risks: If buildBoundarySnapshot fails to enumerate all project files due to permissions, symlinks, or filesystem errors, mutations could go undetected.
If agents modify files during execution then revert before the after-snapshot, mutations would not be detected by the boundary audit.
If allowed_write_roots is misconfigured to include project directories, legitimate project writes would not be detected as violations.
The boundary audit has only been tested with simulated file writes via spawnProviderPrompt callbacks, not with actual full-tool Claude agents.
missing: Stress test evidence of boundary audit with real full-tool Claude agents executing with actual write tools enabled (Bash Edit Write) instead of simulated file writes.
Runtime evidence that boundary violations are properly surfaced to users and prevent PRD generation in real-world usage.
Evidence that buildBoundarySnapshot correctly handles edge cases (large files, binary files, permission errors, symlinks, etc.).
Evidence that agents cannot bypass detection by modifying files then reverting before snapshot.
Evidence that the boundary audit does not produce false positives with allowed_write_roots artifact writes.

result.notes: Independent code review confirms all implementation details cited by the explorer. The boundary snapshot audit is correctly implemented: before/after snapshots, diff logic, blocker generation, and PRD readiness blocking all exist and are tested. However, the demand explicitly requests stress-testing with real full-tool Claude agents, which has not been performed. All tests use simulated file writes via spawnProviderPrompt callbacks, not actual unrestricted Claude agents with Bash/Edit/Write tools. The implementation is theoretically correct but lacks runtime validation with the actual threat model: full-tool Claude agents that could attempt real project mutations.
